etymology.description: semantic 能 (néng, "able") + abbreviated phonetic 炎 (yán, "flame") -> original representation of an onomatopoetic word for flaming -> borrowed for the word 'bear'
etymology.interpretation: Originally, 熊(xióng, "bear") depicted the sound of flames raging, with 炎 (yán, "flame") as the sound component and 能 (néng, "able") representing the original semantic concept of a familiar powerful presence, later shifting to mean 'bear' when 能 (néng, "bear") was borrowed for the animal name.

examples.chinese: 这个熊看起来很友好(這個熊看起來很友好)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè ge xióng kàn qǐlái hěn yǒuhǎo.

examples.chinese: 熊喜欢住在森林里(熊喜歡住在森林裡)。
examples.pinyin: Xióng xǐhuān zhù zài sēnlín lǐ.
examples.english: Bears like to live in the forest.

examples.chinese: 当我在山上徒步时(當我在山上徒步時)，我看到了一个熊(我看到了一个熊)。
examples.pinyin: Dāng wǒ zài shān shàng túbù shí, wǒ kàn dào le yī gè xióng.
examples.english: When I was hiking in the mountains, I saw a bear.
